subject: Geography
title: The Dry Valleys of Antarctica
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The Dry Valleys of Antarctica.

hook.text: Antarctica's Dry Valleys haven't seen rain or snow for nearly 2 million years.

buildup.text: Mountains block ice from flowing in. Fierce winds evaporate any moisture before it lands.

discovery.text: The landscape looks like Mars—barren rock, no ice, no life visible to the naked eye.

twist.text: NASA uses the Dry Valleys to test Mars rovers because the terrain is the closest match on Earth.

climax.text: Even here, microbes survive inside rocks—proving life can endure the most extreme conditions.

punchline.text: The most Mars-like place on Earth still has life hiding in it.

quiz.question: Why does NASA study Antarctica's Dry Valleys?
options: They have the strongest winds on Earth
